originalTitle: Apple is using AI to fix Safari’s extension problem
summary: Apple is trying to solve one of Safari's biggest weaknesses with AI. Safari has long lacked the robust library of extensions that its rivals have, mainly due to the stringent development requirements from Apple. But now, Apple is inviting users to essentially vibe-code their own extensions. In a demo shared by Apple, the company showed […]
content: Apple is trying to solve one of Safari’s biggest weaknesses with AI. Safari has long lacked the robust library of extensions that its rivals have, mainly due to the stringent development requirements from Apple. But now, Apple is inviting users to essentially vibe-code their own extensions. In a demo shared by Apple, the company showed how you can ask Safari to create an extension by describing it. “Save and track cooking recipes from around the web,” the prompt said. “Click the toolbar button to see your saved recipes and add notes to each.” From there, Safari used Apple Intelligence to generate a “Recipe Keeper” extension that’s supposed to do just that. [Image: Apple’s demo showed a vibe-coded “Recipe Keeper” extension. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/safari-extension-vibe.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] If the feature actually works, it could help fill the gap left by the Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox extensions not available on Safari. It should also appeal to those building an arsenal of personal software for themselves with AI.  Safari is playing catch-up with rival browsers in other areas as well. Over the past couple of years, Chrome, Edge, and Firefox have quickly snapped up new AI features, while Safari has largely lagged behind as Apple slowly fed AI into its products. Until now, Safari’s AI toolset has been slim compared to competitors, as it has just offered AI summaries of webpages through a Highlights feature. [Video: Safari AI tab organization] Aside from an extension-making feature, Apple revealed a new AI-powered feature for Safari that will automatically sort your tabs into categories based on what’s in them. That means Safari might organize all of your tabs related to the new running shoes you’re shopping for in a group called “sneakers.” Google rolled out a similar feature for Chrome in 2024, which you could use to right-click a tab and select an “organize” feature to automatically group similar ones. But it looks like Google may have discontinued this feature, as its dedicated webpage redirects you to a Google support page, and I can’t seem to find it in my settings menu. Edge can similarly group tabs based on relevance, while Firefox can generate tab group names using AI. [Image: You can use “Notify Me” to alert you to a product restock or price change. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/wwdc-2026-the-verge-337.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] Apple is adding another familiar feature to Safari as well: the ability to change compromised passwords on your behalf. With the update, Apple’s Passwords app uses Safari and Apple Intelligence to navigate to a website, sign in, and update your account’s passwords. It’s a feature Google first announced for Chrome last year, though it’s only available on “supported websites,” according to the company.  Safari is getting a new “Notify Me” feature, too, which you can use to track changes to a website. Several third-party tools already do this, but Apple is differentiating itself by allowing you to describe the kind of change you’re looking for, like a product restock or price drop, so you won’t get notified over every small change. As competitors rush to add AI-powered browsing features, Apple is being a lot more selective about the kinds of tools it’s adding to Safari. Most AI-powered features just aren’t there yet, and it seems like Apple is taking a slower approach to make sure the kinds of tools embedded have been proven to work.
chineseTitle: 苹果正在使用 AI 来解决 Safari 的扩展问题
chineseSummary: 苹果正试图通过人工智能解决 Safari 最大的弱点之一。 Safari 长期以来缺乏其竞争对手拥有的强大扩展库，这主要是由于苹果严格的开发要求。但现在，苹果正在邀请用户对自己的扩展进行本质上的振动编码。在苹果分享的演示中，该公司展示了 [...]
chineseContent: 苹果正试图通过人工智能解决 Safari 最大的弱点之一。 Safari 长期以来缺乏其竞争对手拥有的强大扩展库，这主要是由于苹果严格的开发要求。但现在，苹果正在邀请用户对自己的扩展进行本质上的振动编码。在 Apple 分享的演示中，该公司展示了如何让 Safari 通过描述来创建扩展。 “保存并跟踪网络上的烹饪食谱，”提示说。 “单击工具栏按钮即可查看您保存的食谱并为每个食谱添加注释。”从那时起，Safari 使用 Apple Intelligence 生成了一个“Recipe Keeper”扩展，该扩展应该可以做到这一点。 [图片：苹果的演示展示了一个振动编码的“Recipe Keeper”扩展。 https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/safari-extension-vibe.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] 如果该功能确实有效，它可以帮助填补 Safari 上不可用的 Google Chrome 和 Mozilla Firefox 扩展留下的空白。它还应该吸引那些利用人工智能为自己构建个人软件库的人。  Safari 在其他领域也正在追赶竞争对手的浏览器。在过去的几年里，Chrome、Edge 和 Firefox 迅速抢占了新的人工智能功能，而随着苹果慢慢将人工智能融入其产品，Safari 基本上落后了。到目前为止，Safari 的人工智能工具集与竞争对手相比还很薄弱，因为它刚刚通过“亮点”功能提供了网页的人工智能摘要。 [视频：Safari AI 选项卡组织] 除了扩展制作功能之外，Apple 还为 Safari 推出了一项新的 AI 驱动功能，该功能将根据选项卡中的内容自动将选项卡分类。这意味着 Safari 可能会组织与您要购买的新跑鞋相关的所有选项卡
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
